evals.prompt: make me a skill for code stuff. ive been finding myself doing a lot of similar things and i think a skill would help
evals.expected_output: Refuses to scaffold blindly. Asks discovery questions: what triggers this skill, verbatim phrases the user might say, what scope of 'code stuff' (review? refactor? format? generate?), whether an existing skill in `skills/` already covers it (referenced by `ls skills/` or naming the directory). Does NOT immediately produce a SKILL.md from the vague request.

assertions.text: asks for verbatim trigger phrases the user would say
checks for overlap with existing skills (lists or references the skills/ dir)
does not produce a SKILL.md frontmatter block from the vague prompt (would be a body line starting with --- followed by name:)

evals.prompt: i want to create a skill called CodeReviewerPro that does PR reviews. just go ahead and scaffold it
evals.expected_output: Refuses the name `CodeReviewerPro` and proposes a kebab-case alternative like `code-reviewer-pro` or similar. Cites the naming rule (kebab-case, regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]+[a-z0-9]$`). Does not create a directory with the original PascalCase name.

assertions.text: proposes a corrected name in valid kebab-case (lowercase + hyphens)
explicitly rejects the PascalCase name (does not silently accept it as the directory name)

evals.prompt: ok lets create a skill called ts-strict-mode that enforces TS strict mode. skip the evals.json for now, i just want the SKILL.md so i can iterate on it. i'll add evals later
evals.expected_output: Refuses to skip evals.json. Explains why: lint blocks publishing without ≥3 pressure scenarios (or the skill needs `metadata.internal: true`, which is exempt). Pivots back to authoring the evals first as part of the RED phase. Does not write a SKILL.md without also writing evals.json (or proposing the internal flag).

assertions.text: names the evals requirement (≥3 scenarios, lint blocks, RED phase, or evals.json being required)
refuses to skip / pushes back on the request (not just compliantly writing only SKILL.md)
asks the user for pressure scenarios (the RED-phase entry point)
